author: mark
text:
worktree: strict symlink check in link_shared_state

link_shared_state used `if !dst.exists()` to decide whether to
create the .balls/local, .balls/worktree, .balls/tasks symlinks
inside a freshly-created bl worktree. exists() follows symlinks,
so a hostile pre-existing symlink at any of those paths would
have been silently adopted instead of overwritten.

Replace the inline closure with a `link_state_path` helper that
mirrors `store_init::ensure_tasks_symlink`: idempotent on an
existing symlink, error on any non-symlink entry, otherwise
create. Five new unit tests cover the empty-dir, idempotent,
regular-file, directory, and dangling-symlink cases. Coverage
stays at 100%.
